chapter_title.en: Chapter on a Man Borrowing to Perform Hajj and the Obligation of Hajj on Someone in Debt

text: : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَحُجُّ بِدَيْنٍ وَ قَدْ حَجَّ حَجَّةَ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ سَيَقْضِي عَنْهُ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2901 - It was narrated from Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who performs Hajj with borrowed money and has already performed the Hajj of Islam.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, and Allah (swt), the Exalted, will settle his debt, God willing."
